title: HSBC Debit Card 2% cashback question
body:
Hi everyone,
Quick question: if I get the HSBC Global Debit Card with 2% cashback on purchases under $100, would it still apply when topping up an Opal card at the station machines? Since the card can’t be used for online transactions, I’m assuming auto top-up won’t work.
My mum and I both use public transport and spend more then $100 a week, so I’m wondering if this could help. Also, what do you all usually use the card for? I can’t see myself getting anywhere near the $2.5k monthly spend needed to maximise the benefits, since most of my purchases are online.
-update
Thanks for all the comments, everyone. I’ve gone ahead and applied for the debit card. Even though it’ll only save me about $10–20 a month, that’s still better than nothing since I wasn’t saving anything before. This is assuming the Woolies/ Coles bypass works, or that the tap on/tap off works directly through the card or Google Pay.
Also the Woolworths eGift card option won't work for us.
